turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey B, so I was looking at our budget review like you asked. Last year we spent $50k on marketing, right? Feels like we should just stick close to that for this year too. What do you think?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, uh, $50k sounds familiar. But didn't we get like, way more leads this year already? Maybe bump it up a bit? I mean, anchoring to last year makes sense, but...

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: True, leads are up, but last year that $50k got us to 500 leads. If we do the same again, probably similar results. No need to overthink it. Hey, speaking of leads, remember that one client who bailed? Total waste.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Haha, yeah, the widget guy. Painful. Okay, fine, $50k for marketing. What about salaries? Last year total was $300k for the team. We hired two more people though.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Two more? Yeah, but their salaries are about the same as the ones they replaced, kinda. Last year $300k covered 10 people, now 12 but whatever, let's say $360k? No, wait, anchor to $300k and add 20% or something. Eh, $350k sounds right.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hmm, 20% feels arbitrary. Last year's average per person was $30k, times 12 is $360k. But you're right, maybe trim it to $340k? We can squeeze 'em. Tangent: coffee budget last year was $2k, that's gotta stay, team loves it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Coffee's sacred, $2k locked in. Okay, rent was $24k last year for the office. We're staying put, so same. No fresh looks needed.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Totally. But utilities jumped last winter, remember? Last year $6k, but with inflation... nah, stick to $6k, hope for the best. What about R&D? $40k last year, and we barely used it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Exactly, $40k again. Why change? We can always roll it over if not needed. Oh man, last year's travel was $10k, mostly that conference flop. Skip conferences this year?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Flop for sure, but networking, you know? Keep $10k, just be pickier. Hey, total budget last year was $450k, revenue $600k. If we anchor expenses to $450k-ish, we're golden.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Let's add it up quick: marketing 50, salaries 350, rent 24, utilities 6, coffee 2, R&D 40, travel 10... uh, 50+350=400, +24=424, +6=430, +2=432, +40=472, +10=482k. A bit over last year's 450k, but close enough.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: 482k? Hmm, maybe cut travel to $5k, back to 477k. Still anchored around there. Imperfect math, but whatever, it'll work. Revenue projections? Last year 600k, so say 650k?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Yeah, 650k feels safe, based on last year. No wild guesses. I think we're good. Send this to the boss?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: One sec, miscellaneous was $8k last year—snacks, swag, whatever. Keep it. Alright, yeah, looks solid. Anchored everything nice and tight.

input.objective: Budget review anchored to last year's numbers
